gP1QrT: List of taxa that represent / are meant in this {subprojectNameSingular} subproject. Examples: synonyms of a single taxonomy or from different taxonomies. A taxon should be used in no more than one subproject.

nX4TyZ: Taxonomies classify objects (e.g. species, biotopes), mostly hierarchically. There is no universally valid way to classify species and biotopes. The genetic composition and distribution of species can change over time. Biotopes change their species composition. Therefore, a taxonomy is never final. It is normal that, depending on context, different taxonomies are used, and in a single project multiple taxonomies may be used simultaneously.

logoutPendingOpsBody: There are still {count} pending local operations. If you sign out now, this local data will be deleted and can no longer be synchronized. Recommendation: Wait until all operations have been synchronized with the server.
logoutLocalDataWipeConfirm:
When signing out, local data on this device will be deleted so the next user does not see old (= your) data. Your data has been synchronized to the server. Therefore it will not be lost and will be available again the next time you sign in.

Sign out now?
